title: Cyclophorus acrostichoides
taxonomy: Cyclophorus acrostichoides is a species of land snail in the family Cyclophoridae. It is classified as a member of the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and order Architaenioglossa. Within the family Cyclophoridae, it belongs to the subfamily Cyclophorinae. The genus Cyclophorus is further categorized under the superfamily Cyclophoroidea. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.

intro: Cyclophorus acrostichoides, also known as the Chinese Cyclophorus, is a type of orchid that is native to the tropical forests of China and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory infections, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclophorus acrostichoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cyclophorus acrostichoides is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
